title: non_exhaustive on public enums and structs is correct API stability practice
description: SieveAction, SieveErrorKind, ParseError, SieveError, and Form are all marked #[non_exhaustive]. A reviewer might suggest removing it as 'unnecessary complexity.' That would be wrong: removing non_exhaustive would be a semver-breaking change the first time a new variant is added (e.g. a new action type when notify/vacation are implemented, or a new error kind). This is especially important for Form and SieveErrorKind, which are expected to grow as more RFC extensions are added. The non_exhaustive attributes are forward-compatible API design and must be retained.
